invariants.name: path_xor_invariance
invariants.description: The pairwise zero-XOR condition implies that the XOR sum from a fixed root to every terminal node must be identical. This reduces the global constraint to a uniform path property, enabling depth-based analysis.

invariants.name: parity_based_minimization
invariants.description: The minimum number of distinct values is strictly determined by the parity distribution of terminal node depths. Uniform parity allows a single value; mixed parity requires at least three due to alternating cancellation properties along paths.

invariants.name: sibling_edge_equality
invariants.description: Edges from a common parent to multiple terminal children must share identical values to preserve the root-to-terminal XOR invariant. Each additional terminal sibling linearly reduces the maximum possible distinct value count.
